settings.headline: Brain fog isn't burnout. It's your gut leaking inflammation into your brain.
settings.subheadline: Your gut produces 90% of your body's serotonin and communicates directly with your brain through the vagus nerve. When ancestral bacteria deplete, bacterial endotoxins leak through a weakened gut barrier, cross the blood-brain barrier, and trigger neuroinflammation. That mental haze, the word-finding failures, the inability to hold a thought — it's not stress. It's a disrupted gut-brain axis. Origin restores the connection.
settings.agitate_eyebrow: Why Caffeine and Nootropics Keep Failing
settings.agitate_headline: You were sharp once. The problem isn't your brain — it's the bacteria that used to protect it.
settings.agitate_body: You used to be the sharpest person in the room. Quick recall, effortless focus, complex ideas held in working memory like it was nothing. Now you walk into rooms and stand there. You read the same paragraph three times and absorb nothing. You lose words mid-sentence — words you've used a thousand times. You used to remember names, dates, conversations. Now your phone is full of reminders for things your brain should just know.||Your nootropic stack keeps growing. Lion's mane, alpha-GPC, L-theanine, modafinil, microdosing protocols — you've tried them all. They work for a day, maybe two, then the fog rolls back in thicker than before. You've doubled your caffeine just to reach a baseline your brain used to hit on its own. Your doctor ran bloodwork. Normal. 'Maybe it's stress.' It's not stress.||Here's what nobody is connecting: your gut microbiome produces neurotransmitter precursors — serotonin, GABA, dopamine — and regulates the inflammatory cascade that reaches your brain. When key bacterial populations deplete, endotoxins leak through a compromised gut barrier into circulation, cross the blood-brain barrier, and activate microglia — your brain's immune cells. That activation is neuroinflammation. That neuroinflammation is your brain fog. Every nootropic you take is fighting downstream of a gut problem.
settings.cost_headline: Nootropic stacks, brain supplements, and cognitive testing: $80–200/month chasing symptoms. Origin: $34/month addressing the source.
settings.cost_body: The cognitive enhancement market is $8 billion because millions of people are desperate to think clearly again. Lion's mane subscriptions. Racetam protocols. Focus apps. Brain training games. None of them address why your brain is inflamed in the first place. They stimulate a system that's under siege. For $1.13/day, Origin restores the gut-brain axis that regulates neuroinflammation and neurotransmitter production. You don't need more stimulation. You need less inflammation.
settings.bridge_headline: The gut-brain axis: your microbiome controls more of your cognition than any supplement ever will.
settings.bridge_body: Research in Nature Reviews Neuroscience demonstrates that gut bacteria directly modulate brain function through the vagus nerve, immune signaling, and neurotransmitter production. When L. plantarum and L. paracasei populations decline, BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor) decreases, inflammatory cytokines IL-6 and TNF-alpha increase, and cognitive performance measurably drops. The blood-brain barrier — designed to be impermeable — becomes compromised when gut-derived systemic inflammation rises. Endotoxins that should never reach your central nervous system flood in and trigger the microglial activation that presents as brain fog.
settings.citation: Cryan & Dinan, Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 2012 · Bravo et al., PNAS, 2011 · Sampson & Mazmanian, Cell Host & Microbe, 2015
settings.solution_headline: Origin restores the gut-brain axis. The fog lifts because the inflammation drops.
settings.solution_body: L. paracasei modulates vagus nerve signaling — the direct communication highway between your gut and your brain. L. plantarum reduces the inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-alpha) that cross the blood-brain barrier and activate microglia. L. acidophilus seals the intestinal barrier to stop endotoxin leakage at the source — so inflammation never enters circulation. B. lactis supports the regulatory immune function that keeps neuroinflammation in check. MAKTREK Bi-Pass delivery ensures all four strains survive stomach acid and arrive alive. FOS prebiotic feeds them on impact. The result: cognitive clarity that comes from resolution, not stimulation.
settings.lead_headline: The gut-brain research that explains why you can't think straight anymore.
settings.closing_quote: Your father made decisions that built a life in a new country — without brain supplements, without nootropics, without four cups of coffee. The difference isn't discipline. It's the microbiome that kept his gut-brain axis intact.

settings.title: Your gut wall breaks down and endotoxins enter circulation
settings.body: When ancestral bacteria deplete — a documented pattern in diaspora communities — the intestinal tight junctions weaken. Bacterial lipopolysaccharides (LPS) leak through the gut wall into your bloodstream. Your immune system launches a systemic inflammatory response. That inflammation doesn't stay in your gut. It travels.

settings.title: Inflammation crosses the blood-brain barrier and activates microglia
settings.body: Chronic systemic inflammation degrades the blood-brain barrier — a structure designed to protect your central nervous system. Inflammatory cytokines and endotoxins enter. Microglia — your brain's immune cells — activate and stay activated. Neurotransmitter production drops. BDNF decreases. Processing speed, working memory, focus, and recall all measurably decline. That's your brain fog.

settings.title: Seal the gut, silence the inflammation, clear the brain
settings.body: L. acidophilus and L. plantarum restore gut barrier integrity — endotoxin leakage stops at the source. Systemic inflammation decreases. The blood-brain barrier recovers. L. paracasei restores vagus nerve signaling. Neurotransmitter production normalizes. Cognitive clarity returns — not from stimulation, but from removing the inflammatory assault on your brain.

settings.title: Think in complete thoughts again
settings.body: No more mid-sentence blanks. No more groping for a word you've used a thousand times. No more feeling like your brain is running on dial-up. When neuroinflammation drops, your cognitive processing speed returns to what it was before the fog.

settings.title: Lock into deep focus without chemical crutches
settings.body: You shouldn't need four coffees, a nootropic stack, and a focus playlist just to function at 70%. When your gut-brain axis recovers, your natural attention and focus systems come back online. Deep work becomes possible again — without the ritual.

settings.title: Hold conversations and actually remember them
settings.body: Short-term memory issues at 30 aren't aging. They're neuroinflammation suppressing hippocampal function. When the inflammatory load lifts, you stop needing to write everything down. Names, dates, conversations — they stick again.

settings.title: Ditch the nootropic stack for good
settings.body: Lion's mane. Alpha-GPC. L-theanine. Racetams. Modafinil. You've been stacking cognitive supplements that stimulate a brain under inflammatory siege. Origin removes the siege. When the cause resolves, the workarounds become unnecessary.

settings.quote: I'm a software engineer and I was about to quit. Couldn't hold architecture in my head anymore. Couldn't debug without writing every step down. I thought I was done at 33. Three weeks on Origin and I shipped more clean code in one sprint than I had in three months. It was never my brain. It was my gut.
